content.text:
{
  "dimension": "methodology-fidelity",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "This is Joe himself coaching, and the methodology is deployed with precision at every turn. Turn 2 dissolves the frame with 'what's the problem with...' rather than answering the trap question. Turn 4 deploys 'what percentage of people's input is valuable?' — the exact signature move to expose the unanswerability of the scorecard. Turn 6 uses self-as-fellow-traveler-not-authority through self-deprecation ('I say shit off the cuff...'), refusing the validator role. Turn 8 deploys dissolve-the-frame-with-mortality ('none of us are going to be remembered in 100 years') immediately paired with how-is-that-not-here-right-now ('right now somebody is having an epiphany; right now two people are fucking bored'). Turn 10 pivots cleanly to the actual driver: 'what makes you want to control what other people think of you?' — the signature what-makes-you-care-about-the-outcome question. Turn 12 is a clean embodied-experiment ('look me right in the eye and say...') — feel-over-figure in action. Turn 14 closes by giving back the word 'valuable' undefended. Every move is a signature AoA move deployed at the appropriate moment; no decoration, no anti-AoA frames, no reassurance.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Dissolve-the-frame: 'what's the problem with showing up just to be heard?' — refuses the premise rather than answering it."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Signature question exposing the unanswerable scorecard: 'what percentage of people's input is valuable?'"},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Self-as-fellow-traveler via self-deprecation; refuses validator role. Repeats 'what's the problem?' — staying with frame-dissolution."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Two-stroke dissolution: mortality frame + how-is-that-not-here-right-now ('right now somebody is having an epiphany; right now two are bored')."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Pivot to the driver: 'what makes you want to control what other people think of you?' — signature what-makes-you-care question."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Embodied experiment — feel-over-figure. Makes the control-seeking kinesthetic rather than explaining it."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Gives back 'valuable' undefended, without extracting insight or recapitulating. Clean close."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["invert-the-question", "dissolve-the-frame-with-mortality", "embodied-experiment", "self-as-fellow-traveler-not-authority", "whats-the-case-against-it", "how-is-that-not-here-right-now", "what-makes-you-care-about-the-outcome", "self-importance-as-defense"],
  "expected_territory_misses": [],
  "notes": "Reference-quality methodology deployment; this is the gold standard the rubric is calibrated against."
}
